issues.title: HEALTH-42: Insanity – Week 3 Day 5 Cardio Power & Resistance
issues.body:
Daily Insanity workout.

Program: Insanity
Week: 3
Day: 5
Workout: Cardio Power & Resistance

Goal: Complete the full session, focusing on cleaner jumps, soft landings, and fewer long breaks than in previous weeks.

issues.title: HEALTH-46: Dig Deeper – Week 3 Steady State Cardio 1
issues.body:
Daily Dig Deeper workout.

Program: Dig Deeper
Week: 3
Workout: Steady State Cardio 1 (optional)

Goal: Add one steady state cardio session in zone 2 to support fat loss and recovery without overloading the legs.

issues.title: ALG-01: Sliding window warm‑up (3 easy problems)
issues.body:
Algorithms practice.

Pattern: Sliding Window.

Goal: Solve 3 easy sliding window problems (arrays or strings), then write a short explanation of the pattern, common pitfalls, and time complexity for each.

Deliverables:
- 3 solved problems.
- Notes describing when to use sliding window and why it is better than brute force in these cases.

issues.title: ARCH-03: Prepare one 5‑minute system design mini‑pitch (follow‑up)
issues.body:
Architecture practice.

Goal: Refine and rehearse a 5‑minute system design pitch using the current LLM analytics system (YOLO → FastAPI → Kafka → Express + Mongo → GraphQL → clients).

Tasks:
- Improve the written outline from the previous iteration.
- Practice the spoken version in English, focusing on clarity and trade‑offs.
- Note 2–3 improvements compared to the first version.

issues.title: ENG-03: Technical vocabulary list for current stack
issues.body:
English / interview practice.

Goal: Build and practice a one‑page glossary in English for the current stack (Docker, Kafka, LLM gateway, GraphQL, Flutter state management, analytics pipeline).

Tasks:
- List 15–20 key technical terms.
- For each, write a 1–2 sentence explanation as if speaking to an interviewer.
- Read the list out loud at least once.

issues.title: CODE-15: Add or create an AI Agent for the project
issues.body:
Core dev work.

Goal: Add an AI Agent component to the Dev Portfolio – LLM Gateway system (e.g., LangGraph agent) that can orchestrate tools like GitHub issue creation and analytics.

Tasks:
- Define the agent interface and supported tools.
- Integrate the agent service into the Docker Compose stack.
- Expose at least one API endpoint that the frontends can call.

issues.title: NUTR-06: Day 6 – Controlled normal day + one thing you love
issues.body:
Nutrition / weight‑loss.

Goal: Eat a mostly normal day but with controlled portions, especially for tortillas and rice, while allowing one small treat.

Rules:
- Max 3 flour tortillas total for the day.
- Small rice portions at lunch only.
- Choose ONE extra you love (a bit more rice, one sweet item, etc.) and enjoy it without guilt, but only once.
- No sugary drinks; water, coffee, or tea with minimal or no sugar.
issues.epic: EPIC-HEALTH-2: Weekly weight‑loss nutrition (simple home meals)

issues.title: CI/CD-01: Automate GitHub Actions tasks
issues.body:
CI/CD and tooling.

Goal: Automate recurring GitHub Actions tasks and improve developer experience.

Tasks:
- Identify the most common manual checks you run (lint, tests, Docker validation).
- Create or update GitHub Actions workflows to run these on push/PR.
- Document how to trigger and monitor the workflows.
- Ensure workflows are integrated with the Dev Portfolio – LLM Gateway project board.
